question: Do the roofs of the buildings now appear to have a golden or copper metallic color?
Does the roof surface exhibit a metallic, reflective finish with specular highlights characteristic of gilded copper?
Has the roof texture changed from matte slate tiles to a smoother appearance or standing-seam metal panels?
Are ornate decorative details, such as ridge caps or cresting, visible on the roofs to enhance the majestic appearance?
Have the roofs of all visible buildings in the complex been consistently updated to the new gilded copper material?
Do the reflections in the pond accurately mirror the new golden/copper color of the roofs?
Are the stone masonry walls of the manor preserved in their original gray/beige color and texture?
Are architectural details such as windows, chimneys, and climbing ivy preserved intact?
Is the original geometry and perspective of the roof structures (gables, dormers, pitch) preserved?
Is the stone bridge in the foreground preserved without alteration?
Are the surrounding landscape elements, including green hills, trees, and grass lawns, preserved?
Is the boundary between the new roofline and the sky clean, free from halos or jagged artifacts?
Is the lighting on the new gilded roofs consistent with the soft, overcast natural light of the scene?
Does the new roofing appear visually integrated with the existing stone architecture without looking 'pasted on'?
Is the image free from unnatural blurriness or resolution degradation in the edited areas?
